params.text:
Why water matters

The body is mostly water, and it needs a steady supply to work properly. Fluids help the blood carry nutrients, keep the kidneys flushing out waste, control body temperature and keep the mind clear. Even mild dehydration can leave someone tired, muddled and unwell.

  • Body: supports circulation, digestion and healthy skin.
  • Brain: helps concentration, mood and alertness.
  • Kidneys: flush out waste and prevent infection.

How much fluid?

Most adults need around 1.5 to 2 litres of fluid a day — roughly six to eight glasses — and more in hot weather, when active, or when unwell with a fever, vomiting or diarrhoea. Individual needs vary, so always follow the person's care plan.

Almost all drinks count towards this, including water, milk, squash, fruit juice, tea and coffee. Foods with a high water content, such as soup, jelly, yoghurt and fruit, help too.

params.taskDescription: Drag each word into the correct gap.
params.textField: Most adults should aim for around *six* to *eight* glasses of fluid a day, which is roughly *1.5* to *2* *litres*.
